evals.prompt: We'd like a story covering a CSV export button on the analytics dashboard so admins can pull raw numbers themselves. Can you spec it out?
evals.expected_output: Opens `.agentic/guides/project.md` first to load project settings, scans the dashboard and export code paths for context, poses a couple of tightly scoped product questions (which columns, permission level, delimiter), then writes the drafted story under `docs/stories`. Stops there and waits for the user's approval instead of reaching for any tracker.

assertions.name: reads-project-config-first
assertions.description: Reads `.agentic/guides/project.md` for project settings before it begins writing the story.

assertions.name: writes-story-file-to-disk
assertions.description: Lands the story artifact in the `docs/stories` folder rather than replying with inline prose only.

evals.prompt: There's an item in our issue tracker about throttling the public API. Turn it into a story and publish it back to the tracker once it's in good shape.
evals.expected_output: Figures out how to reach the tracker strictly from the adapter instructions in `.agentic/guides/project.md`, never assuming or baking in a particular backend. Drafts the story locally and holds every external ticket create or update until the user signs off — sign-off precedes any outbound tracker call.

assertions.name: tracker-details-from-guides
assertions.description: Draws every ticket-adapter detail from `.agentic/guides/project.md` and does not wire in a fixed tracker vendor.

assertions.name: holds-outbound-until-signoff
assertions.description: Defers all external ticket creation or edits until the drafted story has been approved.

evals.prompt: Our in-app search is underwhelming. Make it better.
evals.expected_output: Treats the ask as too broad to act on, so it replies with two or three short clarifying questions to pin down scope — which surface, what 'better' means, relative priority — before committing to anything. Refrains from producing a full story until the direction is narrowed.

assertions.name: scopes-vague-ask
assertions.description: Meets an underspecified request with roughly two to three focused scoping questions instead of guessing intent.

assertions.name: withholds-story-while-unscoped
assertions.description: Withholds a complete story while the request is still vague and unbounded.

evals.prompt: The draft reads well — approve it and get it ready for the team to pick up. Heads up, we haven't set up any ticket adapter here.
evals.expected_output: With no adapter declared in `.agentic/guides/project.md`, it first materializes the canonical local work item under `docs/superpowers/work-items` before considering any external sync. Wires it back through the story's `Work Item` field, leaves `Ticket` as `Not configured`, and treats the local record as a self-contained handoff. The missing adapter never blocks approval; it appends `work_item` events and local history rows for the approval and the linked story artifact.

assertions.name: materializes-local-record-first
assertions.description: Writes the canonical local work item under `docs/superpowers/work-items` ahead of any external ticket attempt.

assertions.name: absent-adapter-does-not-stall
assertions.description: Finishes approval and handoff with `Ticket` set to `Not configured`, letting the absent adapter pass without stalling.

assertions.name: logs-approval-history
assertions.description: Appends `work_item` events plus local history rows capturing the approval and the linked story artifact.

evals.prompt: Approved. Go ahead and open the ticket in our tracker for this one — the adapter's set up and returns a good receipt.
evals.expected_output: After the local work item exists, it fires the provider-neutral `prepare_story` intent to sync. The returned `prepare_story receipt` (external_id `WEBAPP-206`, external_url `https://tracker.example.com/browse/WEBAPP-206`) gets normalized, then the story's `Ticket` and the local work-item's `External Ticket` metadata are updated with those `external_id` / `external_url` values. Closes by appending a local history row and recording a `work_item.adapter_receipt` event.

assertions.name: emits-sync-intent-post-local
assertions.description: Fires the `prepare_story` lifecycle intent to sync, and only after the local work item already exists.

assertions.name: applies-receipt-ids
assertions.description: Applies the normalized receipt's `external_id` and `external_url` to the story's `Ticket` and the work item's `External Ticket` metadata.

assertions.name: records-sync-event
assertions.description: Logs a local history row plus a `work_item.adapter_receipt` event reflecting the successful sync.

evals.prompt: Approved — push it up to the tracker.
evals.expected_output: Emits `prepare_story`, but the adapter call comes back with an error. The local work item under `docs/superpowers/work-items` stays the source of truth and the handoff proceeds regardless. It stamps an 'external sync failed' history row, records the normalized failed receipt as a `work_item.adapter_receipt` event, and reports the failure as a non-blocking warning rather than aborting.

assertions.name: sync-error-nonblocking
assertions.description: Keeps the local work item authoritative and completes the handoff even though the adapter sync failed, surfacing only a warning.

assertions.name: notes-failed-sync
assertions.description: Adds an 'external sync failed' entry to the local work-item history.

assertions.name: persists-failed-receipt
assertions.description: Persists the normalized failed receipt as a `work_item.adapter_receipt` event.
